seed: certainty vs curiosity; order_vs_chaos; rising_signal; bottom_to_top; tide against channels; receding_edges; straight_vs_curved; cage; relief_dread | abstract variation request 20; target batch count 200
title: Fractured Echo of Curiosity
reasoning: The composition captures the emotional tension between certainty and curiosity, illustrated through a carefully selected palette that balances muted tones with sharp accents. The darker edges symbolize boundaries and order, while the scattered lighter shapes represent curiosity and exploration. The intentional disruption of color placement—particularly the clash of dark navy and compressed red in a chaotic pattern—reflects the tension between these two emotions. The main collision occurs in the center, creating a focal point of chaos that draws the eye toward the edges where order prevails. The seed's influence is seen in the island metaphor—structures of solid color contrasting against the unpredictable flow of curiosity's patterns, creating a visual landscape that is both contained and dissolving, echoing the complexity of the emotional narrative.

layout.name: broken_frame
layout.description: A border nearly encloses the square but has deliberate breaks where emotion leaks through.

composition.name: symmetry_vs_break
composition.idea: A nearly mirrored image has one wrong or corrupted side.

referenceExample.title: Rage Entering Calm
referenceExample.pattern: red jagged pixels cutting into blue horizontal bands

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
